Class.name: APTT.Definition.Interaction
APTT.Definition.Messages.AsyncMessage

Method.name: %JSONNew
ValidateResponse

Class.name: APTT.Definition.Messages.Message

Parameter.name: MESSAGECLASS
Parameter.Description: The type of message this is, should be either Ens.Request or Ens.Response

Property.name: Properties
Property.Description: Parsed JSON properties from the test definition file

Method.name: %JSONImportInternal
Method.Implementation: Quit $$$OK

Method.name: GetPropertyDefinition
Method.Description: TODO: MOVE THIS TO A UTIL CLASS

Method.name: %JSONNew
Method.Description:
For some reason the parent object is passed into 'pJSON', so this method should be overridden to pass the correct JSON object from the parent

Ensure the "request" field is the %JSONFIELDNAME of Interaction.MessageRequest

pJSON - The parent JSON object from the test definition file

pParent - The parent ObjectScript OBJECT

Class.name: APTT.Definition.Messages.MessageRequest

Parameter.name: MESSAGECLASS
Parameter.Description: Message classes from the test definition file MUST be a child of Ens.Request

Method.name: %JSONNew
Method.Description:
Parse JSON object into an instance of this MessageRequest class

pJSON - The JSON object to parse

pParent - The parent ObjectScript OBJECT

Method.name: GenerateMessageRequest
Method.Description:
This method generates an object of the message class specified in the test definition file, this value is stored in the ClassName property

It attempts to populate the ..Properties property using the ..PopulateObjectFromJSON method

This method will throw an error if it fails to populate any properties of the message class

Nested object properties will also be instantiated and populated

Method.name: AssertEquals
PopulateObjectFromJSON
Method.Description: A helper method to populate the properties of an object, given the object, and the corresponding JSON object, will throw an error if any properties cannot be set Todo: Move this method to a helper class, along with the reverse method of turning an object to JSON

Class.name: APTT.Definition.Messages.MessageResponse

Method.name: %JSONNew
Method.Description: For some reason the parent object is passed into p1 Ensure the "response" field is the %JSONFIELDNAME of Interaction.MessageRequest

Method.name: ResolveDotPath
Method.Description: A recursive helper method which resolves a path in dot notation e.g. "Name.Addresses[0].Street" and extracts that information from the Response object <property>pObj</property>

Method.name: BuildAllPaths
Method.Description: A recursive helper method to extract all paths from a JSON object

Method.name: ValidateResponse
Method.Description:
This validates the interaction to ensure the response we actually got back from the production (ObjectScript:<property>pMessage</property>) matches the response that we expected from this interaction (JSON:<property>MessageResponse</property>)
May throw an error if there was a problem with validating the response against the expected response
The default behaviour is to check all fields of the actual <property>pMessage</property> match the fields exactly as shown in <property>..Properties</property>, however the validators specified in <property>..Validators</property> will overwrite the default behaviour

Class.name: APTT.Definition.TestCase
APTT.Definition.TestSuite
APTT.Definition.Validators.Base

Parameter.name: VALIDOPTIONS
Parameter.Description: A comma separated list of valid options this validator takes, override in your subclass

Method.name: %OnNew
ValidationError
Method.Implementation: Throw ##class(%Exception.General).%New( "ValidationError for " _ $CLASSNAME(), "-2000",, "Validation error occurred for " _ $CLASSNAME() _ ": " _ pText)

Method.name: Validate
OnValidate
Method.Description: Override this method to validate the value, should return a value of 0 (failure) or 1 (success)

Method.name: hasOption
Method.Implementation: Return ..Options.%IsDefined(pOption)

Method.name: getOption

Class.name: APTT.Definition.Validators.EqualsValidator

Method.name: OnValidate

Class.name: APTT.Definition.Validators.TextValidator

Method.name: OnValidate
Method.Description:
Valid options for this TextValidator: "options": { "contains": ??, "equals": ??, "beginsWith": ??, "endsWith": ??, "caseSensitive": "true", "false", "base64": ?? # A base64 value this field should encode to }
Can specify any combination of the above and they will all be checked /// Override this method to validate the value, should return a value of 0 (failure) or 1 (success)

Method.name: ValidateText

Class.name: APTT.Parser

Method.name: LoadFileAsTestSuite
Method.Description: Parses a file using the test definition JSON model
Method.Implementation:
If ('##class(%File).Exists(pFilepath)) { Throw ##class(%Exception.General).%New("File does not exist",2,,"The test definition file '" _ pFilepath _ "' does not exist") Return "" }
Set file = ##class(%File).%New(pFilepath) Set tSC = file.Open("R") $$$ThrowOnError(tSC)
Set data = ""
while ('file.AtEnd) { Set data = data _ file.Read(,.tSC) $$$ThrowOnError(tSC) }
Do file.Close()
Set testSuite = ##class(APTT.Definition.TestSuite).%New() Set testSuite.SourceFile = pFilepath Set tSC = testSuite.%JSONImport(data) $$$ThrowOnError(tSC)
Return testSuite

Class.name: APTT.ProductionTester
Class.Description:
Auwais's Production Testing Tool
The main entry point for the APTT utility
This class is the main class that enables testing of the production. It will load all of your test definition files and play back the interactions stored within the JSON file
All messages will automatically be created on the fly, you do not need to extend your request or response messages from the %JSON.Adaptor class
To run this tester: - Create a directory with all of your test definition files - Run with Do ##class(APTT.ProductionTester).Run("/path/to/your/test/definitions/directory", "<your production name>")
@author Auwais Hussain (ahussain@intersystems.com) @version 0.6

Parameter.name: PRODUCTION
Parameter.Description: The production to test, this is determined at Runtime so a default value of "None" is specified

Parameter.name: IGNOREPRODUCTIONERRORS
Parameter.Description: We want to perform custom error handling for production errors

Method.name: ColourText
Method.Implementation:
Set cCode = $CASE( pColour, "red":"31", "green":"32", "black":"30", "blue":"34", 1:"30" )
Return $c(27) _ "[" _ cCode _ "m" _ pText _ $c(27) _ "[m"

Method.name: LogColourMessage
Method.Description: Logs a message in a specific colour supported colours: - red - green - black - blue
Method.Implementation: Do ..LogMessage(..ColourText(pText, pColour))

Method.name: Run
OnBeforeProductionStart
OnAfterProductionStart
OnAfterProductionStop
LoadTestDefinitionFiles
Method.Description: Run the parser for a directory of .JSON test files These files MUST end in ".json" to be picked up by this test utility <property>pFileDirectory</property> - The directory in the file system which holds the .json test definitions

Method.name: GetLastMessageHeaderID
ExecuteActions
GetUnixTimestamp
Method.Implementation: Return $ZDT($H, -2)

Method.name: VerifyAsyncMessages
RunOneTestSuite
CheckInteractionErrors
Method.Description: This method checks the errors since the last check to ensure the errors that were expected from this interaction are accounted for

Method.name: CheckErrors

Class.name: APTT.Recorder

UDLText.name: T
UDLText.Content: // Todo: Move to UTIL class, DUPLICATE CODE.

Method.name: GetPropertyDefinition
GetLastMessageHeaderID
Method.Implementation:
&sql(SELECT TOP 1 id INTO :lastid FROM Ens.MessageHeader WHERE SourceConfigName=:pFrom AND TargetConfigName=:pTo ORDER BY id DESC)
Return lastid

Method.name: ClassType
Method.Description: Compute a property type given its class name Returns either "primitive", "list", "array", "object" or "json"

Method.name: TestConverter
Method.Implementation:
Set a = ##class(APTT.Example.Request).%New() Set a.Name = "Testing" Set a.Age = 183 Set a.Gender = "M" Do a.ContactNumbers.SetAt(1938293, "Home") Do a.FavouriteColours.Insert("Blue") Do a.FavouriteColours.Insert("Red") Do a.FavouriteColours.Insert("Green") Set address1 = ##class(APTT.Example.Model.Address).%New() Set address1.HouseNumber = 381 Do address1.Streets.Insert("FakeStreet 1") Set a.MainAddress = address1
Set address2 = ##class(APTT.Example.Model.Address).%New() Set address2.HouseNumber = 34 Do address2.Streets.Insert("FakeStreet2 For OtherAddress") Do a.OtherAddresses.Insert(address1) Do a.OtherAddresses.Insert(address2) Do a.NamedAddresses.SetAt(address1, "Home")
Write ..PopulateJSONFromObject(a).%ToJSON()

Method.name: PopulateJSONFromObject
MakeBooleanPrompt
Method.Description: pAcceptedAnswers is a comma-delimited list of potential answers

Method.name: Record
RecordOneTest
Method.Implementation:
Set lastID = ..GetLastMessageHeaderID(pTarget, "EnsLib.Testing.Process")
Write !,"Last message ID found: " _ lastID
Write !,"**Please test the component using the management portal.."
Set latestID = lastID While (latestID = lastID) { Set latestID = ..GetLastMessageHeaderID(pTarget, "EnsLib.Testing.Process") Hang 2 }
/// A new message was detected, attempt to parse the input and output of this message /// into JSON to be stored in the test definition file Set reqMsgRequestHeader = ##class(Ens.MessageHeader).%OpenId(..GetLastMessageHeaderID("EnsLib.Testing.Process", pTarget)) Set reqMsgBodyClass = reqMsgRequestHeader.MessageBodyClassName Set reqMsgBodyID = reqMsgRequestHeader.MessageBodyId Set reqObj = $CLASSMETHOD(reqMsgBodyClass, "%OpenId", reqMsgBodyID) Set reqJSON = ..PopulateJSONFromObject(reqObj)
Set respMsgRequestHeader = ##class(Ens.MessageHeader).%OpenId(..GetLastMessageHeaderID(pTarget, "EnsLib.Testing.Process")) Set respMsgBodyClass = respMsgRequestHeader.MessageBodyClassName Set respMsgBodyID = respMsgRequestHeader.MessageBodyId Set respObj = $CLASSMETHOD(respMsgBodyClass, "%OpenId", respMsgBodyID) Set respJSON = ..PopulateJSONFromObject(respObj)
/// Print the test results out to the user Write !,!,"Request Message",! Set formatter = ##class(%JSON.Formatter).%New()
Do formatter.FormatToString(reqJSON, .reqFormatted) Write reqFormatted
Write !,!,"Response Message",!
Do formatter.FormatToString(respJSON, .respFormatted) Write respFormatted
Set accept = ..MakeBooleanPrompt("Accept test [A] or Reject [R]: ", "A,R")
If (accept = "R") { Write !,"**Test has been rejected" Return "" }
Set newLastID = ..GetLastMessageHeaderID(pTarget, "EnsLib.Testing.Process")
If newLastID = lastID { Write !,"No new messages detected..." Return "" }
Read !,"Enter a name for this test: ", testName
Set interaction = {} Set interaction.name = testName Set interaction.request = {} Set interaction.request.class = reqMsgBodyClass Set interaction.request.properties = reqJSON
Set interaction.response = {} Set interaction.response.class = respMsgBodyClass Set interaction.response.properties = respJSON
Return interaction
